question: What specific documents are required to prove eligibility for the 3-year Lateral Entry program?
answer:
To prove eligibility for the 3-year Lateral Entry B.E./B.Tech. program, you'll need:

•   A pass certificate in a Diploma in the appropriate branch of Engineering/Technology from the State Board of Technical Education and Training, Tamil Nadu, or its equivalent.
•   OR, a pass certificate in a recognized Bachelor's Degree in Science of minimum 3 years duration with a 10+2+3 pattern, having studied core Mathematics as one of the subjects at the degree level. Note that candidates with degrees other than Bachelor of Science are not eligible.

Also, when you produce a grade certificate, you must also provide the actual marks; otherwise, only the minimum marks applicable to the grades in the subjects will be considered.

Do you have your Diploma or B.Sc. degree already?

evaluation.reason: The BOT response accurately describes the eligibility criteria for lateral entry, found on other sources, for similar programs. The source text has basic eligibility, but not lateral entry requirements.

question: Does the department offer any industry-recognized certifications related to IoT Security?
answer:
Indeed, MSAJCE offers industry-recognized certifications through our Embedded & IoT Centre and the CISCO Networking Academy.

•   Embedded & IoT Centre: Provides hands-on experience in building IoT systems using Arduino and Raspberry Pi.
•   CISCO Networking Academy: Offers unique hands-on learning in networking, security, and cloud technologies using simulation tools and professional certifications, like CISCO Certified Network Associate (CCNA).

question: Does the program offer any opportunities for students to participate in national or international cybersecurity competitions?
answer:
MSAJCE supports participation in cybersecurity competitions via the Coding Club and the Cyber Security Academy.

• Coding Club: The club represents the college at intercollegiate competitions and participates in events like ACM-ICPC, Google Code Jam, Google Summer of Code, and other online coding events.
• Cyber Security Academy: Enhances skills through Capture The Flag (CTF) challenges. These challenges help you practice penetration testing, forensics, and cryptography using platforms like Hack The Box, TryHackMe, and Over The Wire.

Interested in learning about our anti-ragging policies?
